config.kitMics: Schoeps MK41, Schoeps MK4. Schoeps MiniCMIT, Schoeps KMIT, DPA 6060, DPA 6061
config.kitTxRx: Wisycom MCR54, MTP61 x4, MTP41 x4

1.notes: - No Dia - mostly movements and breaths will be important. Plant mics where possible for movements.
1.description:
An artist lives here, there's no doubtabout it. Dozens of canvases lean against the walls of the small living room.
In the distance, one can glimpse endless fields, colorful bouquets of flowers, and radiant sunrises.
But the colorful paintings are dusty and cluttered with grey landscapes that become increasingly abstract from picture to picture.
A brush is applied. Pale colors spread across the canvas, forming abstract structures.
In front of this almost black and white picture on the easel stands MARGRET(78), a petite lady with faded red hair.
Now we realize how gloomy the whole room is. Everything looks gray and lifeless. The thin curtains in front of the window
have long since yellowed. Stacks of books and dust bunnies are piled up in the strangest places. A dead moth lies
gathering dust under the table lamp.
Hardly any sunlight penetrates the apartment; instead, there is the screaming of angry children, aggressive barking of dogs,
and thunder and rumbling from the backyard. Eerie shadows are cast behind the thin curtains.
Startled, Margret looks up from her picture, wraps her cardigan tighter around her thin body, and steps forward to close the
heavy, dark velvet curtains.
1.problems: Specific lighting through window mentioned many times (careful with shadows).

2.notes:
- Margret mutters to herself - not "dialog" but unscripted vocal expression expected. Margret later says "get out of here".
- Multi room scene, may need more plant mics if camera transitions rooms in single shot
2.description: When Margret wakes up, she's still clutching the umbrella tightly. Cautiously, she ventures out of the bedroom again. Surely it was all just a dream. Just to be sure, she checks in the corner, but there's only chaos. She mutters to herself as she does so. But she immediately notices that someone has opened the curtains. The unfamiliar sunlight dazzles her. She reaches out her hand to shield herself and tiptoes to the window. As she resolutely draws the curtains closed, her gaze falls upon a few green splashes of paint on the fabric. The traces of paint continue on the floor and lead directly to the canvas. And, oh dear, a small shadow made of colorful pigments is frolicking about on it, having just destroyed Margret's grey picture with its green handprints. Margret gasped in shock, the shadow froze. They glanced at each other briefly, then Margret darted towards him, waving her hands. "Get out of here!" The shadow fled. She has to save her picture! She paints over the colorful marks with white paint, but only smears everything even more. She sighs. First things first, breakfast. But as she opens the refrigerator door, an olive pit falls on her head. She looks up in disbelief. Surely that's not really the shadow sitting on top, with the half-empty jar of olives next to her. Completely annoyed, she slams the door shut and takes a pear from the fruit bowl instead. She strides purposefully towards the canvas. She'll just have to start over. She fetches a new canvas from the corner, but as she does, the shadow throws one brush after another onto the floor.
2.problems: - Multi room, check camera movements and make sure to plan for any multi room takes

4.notes: - Second half of scene utilises far more VFX, can record sound but will be very sound design heavy.
4.description:
Margret stands before a canvas. Completely empty, white, and full of possibilities. The curtains are finally closed. It's quiet around her. Everything as usual.
Deliberately, she lifts the brush with her right hand and the palette with her left. Dip. Breathe. Then she guides the brush to the canvas, very gently and... YELLOW?
Surprised, the paint palette slips from her hand. Before Margret can realize what's happening, the shadow darts out, lunges towards her, and catches the palette at the last moment. Margret looks at the suspended palette. Then at the shadow that slowly peeks out from behind it. She hesitates. Looks at the canvas, at the ghastly yellow, then back at the palette. An idea spreads within Margret, warm, gentle, and shy. Carefully, Margret reaches for the palette; the shade allows her to do so.
MARGRET: (breathing) Thanks.
The shadow tilts its head as if surprised by the small word. Then he watches as Margret dips her brush into the yellow paint again, this time quite deliberately.
And Margret paints. She reaches for bright colors: green, pink, red. The shadow steps onto an open tube of paint and squeezes a vibrant blue onto her palette.
Margret's radiance returns, the paintbrush seems to develop a mind of its own. His work seems to be done, and so the shadow creeps, silently and stealthily, to the window. A jerk, and a ray of light breaks through the thick curtains. The sun shines over the chaos, the canvases, the splashes of paint, and finally over
Margret's hands. She lets it happen. The shadow writhes in the light, glittering and shining and spinning. It whirls around, losing first one, then two, then three particles. It swirls directly over the pigment container from which it initially emerged. Slowly, it dissolves, and the pigments trickle back into the container.

5.notes: Silent mutterings - ensure good lav placement.
5.description:
Margret finishes her artwork, a colorful bouquet of flowers, and breathes a sigh of relief. In the light, her cheeks glow bright red.
MARGRET: Just look!
She turns around, beaming with pride, but there's no sign of the shadow. There's no one there for Margret to show her picture to. She looks under the dresser. Nothing. In the refrigerator. Nothing. Then Margret shrugged. That's what she wanted all along, isn't it? Her peace and quiet. Now she has it again.
MARGRET (CONT'D) (muttering): It was about time you disappeared.
Was that it? Margret is very unsure. But what choice does she have but to return to her routine?

6.notes: No dia - potential plant in Fridge if required on the day
6.description:
Margret gets up and walks into the kitchen. There's a half-eaten pear in the fruit bowl. Margret sighs.
Margret looks in the refrigerator. There's one last olive in the jar. She doesn't touch it. Margret stands in front of the canvas. The flower painting radiates towards her, full of color, full of joy – but something is wrong with Margret. She looks around her studio, everything so gray, everything so still. Then her gaze falls on the glittering particles of color, dancing in the light. Light. Sunlight.
Margret examines the gap in the curtain. Cautiously, she peers through it, outwards. For the first time, really looking at it properly.
We don't see everything that's going on, but we do see Margret's features. First thoughtful, then determined. She turns around with a jerk.

7.description:
Motion Sequence:
The traces of chaos continue:
A half-eaten pear in the fruit basket.
Margret closes the open curtain.
Dirty brushes are standing in a teacup instead of in brush water.
Margret closes the open curtain.
Black, white, and grey paint tubes were all thrown on the ground.
Margret, annoyed, pulls the curtain closed.
The colorful paint tubes and pigment pots are scattered haphazardly all over the table; some have already
leaked.
Margret, very annoyed, pulls the curtain closed.

8.notes:
- likely a lot of VFX
- multi room

8.problems:
- multi room / check camera movements
- could be off camera direction

9.notes:
Margret has no scripted dialog, but could improvise a response.
Likely no plants needed. Can be prepped and added on set if required.
9.description:
Now we finally see everything Margret saw.
Magnificent wildflowers are blooming, very close by. Then slowly we recognize more of the surroundings. We see a well-tended little garden, wrinkled hands diligently watering the flowers.
An older lady, Hilde, in the courtyard, she is smiling. And then, Margret. She steps through the front door into the courtyard. Very carefully, one foot after the other. As if she had to learn to walk again.
The canvas is tucked under her arm. The one with the bouquet of flowers. Hilde looks up, examines, recognizes, and marvels. A bouquet of flowers in her hands.
HILDE: Margret?
The two women approach each other hesitantly. They meet amidst flowers, both painted and blooming.
9.problems: EXT scene recorded outdoors. Do not use MK4 (limit room splash).
